title: Implementation Trail
description: Versioned, machine-readable record of a preferred implementation ordering (waves) for a selected task/topic scope, with first-class narrative rationale. One trail artifact is the single source of truth; Markdown/TUI views are deterministic projections of this document and are never persisted as a second source.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the document conforms to. Consumers MUST reject documents whose major version they do not understand.

trail_id.description: Stable identity of the trail across refreshes. Immutable once created; artifact content versions of the same handle share this id.

title.description: Short human title of the trail (e.g. 'Gate framework landing order').

owner.description: The task that owns the artifact handle (the artifact substrate supports only task-owned artifacts). For cross-topic / ad-hoc trails this owner is explicitly user-selected at creation.

scope.description: What the trail covers and how that scope was selected.

initiating_task.description: Task from which the trail was invoked, when different from owner.

topics.description: Canonical topic-group keys (root task ids) intersected by this trail. Trail membership NEVER redefines a task's canonical topic: `anchor` stays the one group key, and this list is a projection, not an assignment.

selection_note.description: Narrative: how/why this scope (and, for ad_hoc/multi_topic, this owner) was chosen.

generation.description: Provenance of this trail version: who generated it, from what repository state, and over which inputs. The input digest is the anchor of staleness detection.

agent_string.description: Code agent/model that produced the analysis, e.g. 'claudecode/fable5'.

skill.description: Skill (and optional refresh subskill) that produced this version.

project_revision.description: Source revision(s) the analysis observed, keyed by logical project name (e.g. {"aitasks": "9692ff5dc"}).

input_digest.description: Digest over the normalized input snapshot (member/observed task frontmatter subset: status, depends, gates, boardcol/boardidx presence, plan-file identity). Recompute-and-compare is the staleness primitive; the normalization procedure is versioned with the schema so digests are comparable only within a schema version.

inputs.description: The tasks/files whose normalized state fed the digest — presence-tracked so a deleted input is itself detectable drift.

freshness.description: Last computed staleness verdict. Consumers recompute the input digest on demand and record named drift reasons here; timestamps and board repaints alone are never drift.

drift_reasons.description: Named, structured reasons the trail no longer matches reality (empty when state=current). A refresh consumes these to make the re-analysis targeted, not a blind re-read.

refresh_recommended_because.description: Narrative: why a refresh is (or is not yet) worth running, in terms a human triaging the board can act on.

narrative.description: First-class prose analysis. A trail is an explained recommendation, not a bare ranked list: this section carries the overall reasoning, and every wave/entry carries its own. Projections (Markdown/TUI) render these fields verbatim.

problem_statement.description: What question this trail answers and why now.

recommendation_summary.description: The headline answer: which tasks should land next, in what waves, and the one-paragraph why.

method_note.description: How the analysis was performed and its limits (what was read, what was NOT verified).

caveats.description: Global caveats separating observed facts from agent judgment.

waves.description: Ordered landing waves. Order within the array is presentation order; `ordinal` is the authoritative sequence and MUST be strictly increasing.

relations.description: Typed directed edges between referenced tasks. `provenance: fact` edges mirror real recorded constraints (e.g. `depends`); `provenance: advisory` edges are the trail's recommendation only and MUST NOT be written back to task metadata by the analysis.

observations.description: Findings that shape the ordering but are not themselves trail members — broken baselines, in-flight conflicting work, stale premises, environment risks. Observed facts, each backed by evidence.

statement.description: Narrative: what was observed and why it matters for the ordering.

exclusions.description: Work that was considered and deliberately left out of the ordering, with the narrative reason it does not block. Prevents re-litigating the same candidates on every refresh.

evidence.description: Snapshot of the observations' sources. Evidence records identify where a fact came from; they carry identifiers and concise summaries, never copied task/plan bodies (tasks stay the single source of their own descriptions).

ref.description: Locator for the source (path, task ref, command line). Not a content copy.

rendering_hints.description: Advisory presentation preferences (e.g. preferred badge glyphs, collapse defaults). Renderers may ignore them; hints never duplicate canonical content.

task_ref.description: Project-qualified task reference using the cross-repo notation (aidocs/framework/cross_repo_references.md): '<project>#<id>' where <project> is a logical registry name and <id> is a bare task id ('835' or '835_3', no 't' prefix — the canonical stored form; the tolerated 't' input form is normalized on write). The same shape refers to local and registered cross-repo tasks; the local project uses its own logical name.

purpose.description: Narrative: what this wave achieves as a unit and why it forms a boundary (what it unblocks or de-risks for later waves).

why_now.description: Narrative: why this wave should run at this position rather than earlier/later.

consequence_of_delay.description: Narrative: what it costs to postpone this wave (rework, blocked work, decaying context).

topic.description: The task's canonical topic-group key (its anchor root) at generation time. Recorded for projection only; the trail never reassigns it.

position.description: 1-based order within the wave; strictly increasing per wave.

snapshot.description: Point-in-time copy of the task's ordering-relevant metadata at generation. Display convenience + drift anchor; the task file remains the source of truth.

rationale.description: Narrative: why this task is in the trail, at this position — the motivation, not a restatement of the task title.

expected_outcome.description: Narrative: what landing this task is expected to produce/unblock.

why_order_matters.description: Narrative: what specifically breaks or degrades if this entry is taken out of order (empty when order is a mild preference).
